items.title: Iran War Forces Japan to Rethink Its Love For Plastic

Japan loves plastic. But the war in Iran is forcing it to rethink its love for the cheap material and the feasibility of individually wrapping every product.

Alice French explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Война США и Ирана : какие новые удары , продолжаются ли переговоры

The question of whether Ukraine can, through diplomatic or other efforts, prevent fuel supplies to Russia from neutral countries sounds like a historical joke. A country like Russia, whose economy has always been focused on exporting its own energy resources, is now scrambling to find fuel somewhere and somehow increase its ability to import fuel. So has Ukraine prevented this yet? Ukraine already has; we have already done this. I think that if Yeltsin had known that 20-plus years later Russia, instead of exporting energy resources, would be importing them because it had asininely decided to unleash a war, Yeltsin would have chosen a different successor. From answers to journalists’ questions. (3/3)

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?

As US President Donald Trump flew home from a fractious Nato summit in Turkey, he was poised to resume the war with Iran, whose leaders he labelled “sick” and “scum”. Trump also complained about European leaders’ failure to spend enough on arms, support him in Iran and recognise the need for the US to take control of Greenland. The sense of rising global conflict has been palpable this week.

items.title: FREYA. This is our Ukrainian anti-ballistic system. This project is an anti-ballistic system that should be an analogue of Patriot in terms of intercepting ballistic targets, but one that is easier to mass-produce and less expensive. This is a European model. Our first meeting on this topic will take place in France, and it will happen in the near future. Our task was to bring together leaders, companies, and national security advisors – and to present the relevant project to countries that can help Ukraine implement it very quickly. If the leaders, their production capacities, and their companies support FREYA, then FREYA will come to life very soon. Patriots are number one. We are grateful to the United States and to the Europeans who have helped us with these systems. There is SAMP/T NG – the new generation of French systems. Is it an analogue of Patriot? Yes. And we very much hope that, thanks to our agreements with France, we will receive the corresponding systems. But with both Patriots and SAMP/T, it is a matter of time. Production is very limited, and the waiting lists are very long. That is why we have started building our own European project called FREYA. I very much count on the success of this project. From answers to journalists’ questions. (2/3)

items.title: Fed Chairman Warsh names members of monetary policy task forces
Скандал УПА і Волинню - Сибіга прокоментував відносини з Польщею

Right now, we have a window of opportunity. Ukraine has become stronger both on the battlefield and in the skies. Everyone sees these results made possible thanks to our warriors in the first place. I am very grateful to them. Putin – whether he knows this or not – is certainly receiving information and understands that right now he has no advantage. He has ballistic missiles; there are indeed absolutely tragic and horrific strikes against the civilian population of our state, but this is the only advantage he has left. There is nothing else. And everyone sees this. We spoke with President Trump, and I also met with many different leaders during the summit. Even with leaders of countries with whom I had not had a meeting before. They all note one thing: Ukraine has become stronger. From answers to journalists’ questions. (1/3)
